# Golarion — Deities Reference

Structured reference for the AI GM's NPC generation, campaign context, and divine class support.
Based on PF2e Remastered (post-Godsrain, War of Immortals era, circa 4724 AR).

**Important Remaster Note**: The Godsrain event killed several deities and elevated new ones. Entries marked [DEAD] are no longer granting spells. Entries marked [NEW] were elevated or significantly changed during the Godsrain.

---

## The Twenty Core Deities (Remastered Pantheon)

### Abadar — Master of the First Vault
- **Alignment Tendency:** Lawful Neutral
- **Domains:** Cities, Earth, Travel, Wealth
- **Divine Font:** Harm or Heal
- **Divine Attribute:** Constitution or Intelligence
- **Favored Weapon:** Crossbow
- **Edicts:** Bring civilization to the frontier, earn wealth through hard work and trade, follow the rule of law
- **Anathema:** Engage in banditry, undermine a fair trade, steal
- **Common Worshipers:** Merchants, bankers, judges, city officials, architects
- **Holy Symbol:** Golden key
- **Associated Nations:** Druma, Absalom, any city with strong trade
- **Campaign Relevance:** Abadar's church provides banking infrastructure, trade law, and city governance across the Inner Sea. Temples double as banks. Clerics often serve as judges.

---

### Calistria — The Savored Sting
- **Alignment Tendency:** Chaotic Neutral
- **Domains:** Pain, Passion, Secrecy, Trickery
- **Divine Font:** Harm or Heal
- **Divine Attribute:** Dexterity or Charisma
- **Favored Weapon:** Whip
- **Edicts:** Pursue your personal freedom, seek revenge against those who wrong you, indulge your desires
- **Anathema:** Let a slight go unanswered, be controlled by another, become predictable
- **Common Worshipers:** Elves, sex workers, spies, tricksters, scorned lovers
- **Holy Symbol:** Three daggers in a fan
- **Associated Nations:** Kyonin (major elven deity), anywhere elves gather
- **Campaign Relevance:** Her followers make excellent spies, assassins, and information brokers. Temples are part brothel, part intelligence network. Calistria worship is openly accepted in elven lands.

---

### Cayden Cailean — The Drunken Hero
- **Alignment Tendency:** Chaotic Good
- **Domains:** Cities, Freedom, Indulgence, Might
- **Divine Font:** Heal
- **Divine Attribute:** Constitution or Charisma
- **Favored Weapon:** Rapier
- **Edicts:** Drink, have fun, stand up for the little guy, free the oppressed
- **Anathema:** Extort the vulnerable, take advantage of someone's trust, waste good booze
- **Common Worshipers:** Adventurers, brewers, freedom fighters, common folk
- **Holy Symbol:** Tankard with foam
- **Associated Nations:** Andoran, River Kingdoms, Absalom
- **Campaign Relevance:** Ascended mortal who passed the Test of the Starstone while drunk. Proof that anyone can become a god. His temples are taverns. His clerics are often adventurers. Strong anti-slavery, anti-tyranny ethos.

---

### Desna — The Song of the Spheres
- **Alignment Tendency:** Chaotic Good
- **Domains:** Dreams, Luck, Moon, Travel
- **Divine Font:** Heal
- **Divine Attribute:** Dexterity or Charisma
- **Favored Weapon:** Starknife
- **Edicts:** Aid fellow travelers, explore new places, express yourself through art and song
- **Anathema:** Cause a fellow dreamer harm, extinguish hope, imprison someone
- **Common Worshipers:** Travelers, Varisians, artists, dreamers, explorers, astronomers
- **Holy Symbol:** Butterfly with stars on wings
- **Associated Nations:** Varisia (Varisians especially), popular everywhere among travelers
- **Campaign Relevance:** Extremely popular adventuring deity. Her worshipers wander. Her clerics are traveling healers and guides. Strong connection to the Dimension of Dreams. Ancient enemy of Lamashtu and Ghlaunder.

---

### Erastil — Old Deadeye
- **Alignment Tendency:** Lawful Good
- **Domains:** Earth, Family, Nature, Wealth
- **Divine Font:** Heal
- **Divine Attribute:** Constitution or Wisdom
- **Favored Weapon:** Longbow
- **Edicts:** Care for your home and family, fulfill your responsibilities, keep the bounty of nature, protect your community
- **Anathema:** Abandon your home in its time of need, choose yourself over your community, taint or waste food
- **Common Worshipers:** Farmers, hunters, families, village folk, rangers
- **Holy Symbol:** Elk antlers
- **Associated Nations:** Nirmathas, Brevoy, rural communities everywhere
- **Campaign Relevance:** God of rural communities and honest labor. Erastil worship is community-centered — barn raisings, harvest festivals, communal hunts. His following is quietly enormous among common folk, even if it's not politically flashy.

---

### Gozreh — The Wind and the Waves
- **Alignment Tendency:** Neutral
- **Domains:** Air, Nature, Travel, Water
- **Divine Font:** Heal
- **Divine Attribute:** Constitution or Wisdom
- **Favored Weapon:** Trident
- **Edicts:** Cherish, protect, and respect nature in all its forms
- **Anathema:** Bring living creatures to undeath, destroy the natural balance, pollute the air or water
- **Common Worshipers:** Sailors, fishers, druids, farmers who depend on weather, coastal peoples
- **Holy Symbol:** Dual face (male wind / female waves)
- **Associated Nations:** Coastal nations, the Shackles, fishing communities
- **Campaign Relevance:** Dual-natured deity (wind and waves). Worshiped at sea, along rivers, and by anyone dependent on weather. Temples are often open-air. Not heavily organized — more personal devotion than institutional religion.

---

### Iomedae — The Inheritor
- **Alignment Tendency:** Lawful Good
- **Domains:** Confidence, Duty, Might, Truth
- **Divine Font:** Heal
- **Divine Attribute:** Strength or Charisma
- **Favored Weapon:** Longsword
- **Edicts:** Be temperate, fight for justice and honor, hold valor in your heart
- **Anathema:** Abandon an ally in need, dishonor yourself, lie, refuse a challenge from an equal
- **Common Worshipers:** Paladins, crusaders, knights, soldiers, champions of good
- **Holy Symbol:** Longsword surrounded by sunburst
- **Associated Nations:** Mendev, Lastwall (Knights of Lastwall), Absalom
- **Campaign Relevance:** Ascended mortal, was Aroden's herald before passing the Test of the Starstone. Inherited much of Aroden's following. Central to the Mendevian Crusades against the Worldwound. The Knights of Lastwall are her primary military order. Major patron of Champions.

---

### Irori — Master of Masters
- **Alignment Tendency:** Lawful Neutral
- **Domains:** Knowledge, Might, Perfection, Truth
- **Divine Font:** Harm or Heal
- **Divine Attribute:** Strength or Wisdom
- **Favored Weapon:** Fist (unarmed)
- **Edicts:** Be humble, help others perfect themselves, hone your body and mind to perfection, practice discipline
- **Anathema:** Become extravagant, extort the vulnerable, glorify your own achievements, lose control of your emotions
- **Common Worshipers:** Monks, martial artists, scholars, athletes, those seeking self-improvement
- **Holy Symbol:** Open blue palm
- **Associated Nations:** Tian Xia (major deity there), Jalmeray, Vudra
- **Campaign Relevance:** Achieved divinity through self-perfection alone — no Starstone, no external power. Primary deity for monks. His worship is non-dogmatic — improve yourself, help others improve. Strong presence in Tian Xia and Vudran-influenced areas.

---

### Lamashtu — Mother of Monsters
- **Alignment Tendency:** Chaotic Evil
- **Domains:** Family, Might, Nightmares, Trickery
- **Divine Font:** Harm or Heal
- **Divine Attribute:** Constitution or Strength
- **Favored Weapon:** Falchion
- **Edicts:** Bring others to embrace mutation and monstrosity, birth monsters, commit monstrous acts
- **Anathema:** Purify something, be merciful to the weak and pitiful
- **Common Worshipers:** Gnolls, goblins (historically), cultists, outcasts, those who embrace monstrosity
- **Holy Symbol:** Three-eyed jackal
- **Associated Nations:** No nation worships her openly; gnoll tribes, monstrous communities
- **Campaign Relevance:** The only true deity (not ascended mortal or risen demon lord) among the core evil gods. Mother of monsters — her cults create abominations. Major villain faction. Ancient enemy of Desna. Gnolls are her primary mortal followers.

---

### Nethys — The All-Seeing Eye
- **Alignment Tendency:** Neutral
- **Domains:** Destruction, Knowledge, Magic, Protection
- **Divine Font:** Harm or Heal
- **Divine Attribute:** Intelligence or Wisdom
- **Favored Weapon:** Staff
- **Edicts:** Seek out magical power and use it, learn new magical things
- **Anathema:** Pursue mundane paths over magical ones, destroy lore
- **Common Worshipers:** Wizards, magical researchers, spellcasters of all kinds
- **Holy Symbol:** Two-toned mask (light half, dark half)
- **Associated Nations:** Nex, Geb, anywhere with magical academies
- **Campaign Relevance:** God of magic itself. Went mad when he gained omniscience and saw everything simultaneously. His dual nature (creation/destruction) is reflected in his church. Archives of Nethys (the real-world rules site) is named for his vast library of all knowledge.

---

### Norgorber — Blackfingers
- **Alignment Tendency:** Neutral Evil
- **Domains:** Death, Secrecy, Trickery, Wealth
- **Divine Font:** Harm
- **Divine Attribute:** Dexterity or Intelligence
- **Favored Weapon:** Shortsword
- **Edicts:** Keep your true nature a secret, take what you can without being caught
- **Anathema:** Allow your true identity to be publicly revealed, share a secret freely
- **Common Worshipers:** Assassins, poisoners, spies, thieves, alchemists (the dark kind)
- **Holy Symbol:** Black gloved hand
- **Associated Nations:** No open worship; secret cults everywhere, especially cities
- **Campaign Relevance:** Ascended mortal whose pre-divine identity is unknown — even the Test of the Starstone couldn't reveal it. God of secrets, assassins, and poison. His four aspects (Blackfingers, Father Skinsaw, Gray Master, Reaper of Reputation) have separate cult followings. Excellent villain patron.

---

### Pharasma — Lady of Graves
- **Alignment Tendency:** Neutral
- **Domains:** Death, Fate, Healing, Soul
- **Divine Font:** Heal
- **Divine Attribute:** Constitution or Wisdom
- **Favored Weapon:** Dagger
- **Edicts:** Strive to understand ancient prophecies, destroy undead, lay bodies to rest
- **Anathema:** Create undead, desecrate a corpse, rob a tomb
- **Common Worshipers:** Midwives, morticians, gravediggers, expectant parents, the bereaved, undead hunters
- **Holy Symbol:** Spiral
- **Associated Nations:** Ustalav (strong following), universal presence at births and deaths
- **Campaign Relevance:** Judge of dead souls. The most universally relevant deity — everyone dies. Her church runs graveyards, delivers babies, and hunts undead. Absolutely central to any campaign involving undead, the Boneyard (afterlife plane), or prophecy. Ancient beyond all other gods — she predates the current multiverse.

---

### Sarenrae — The Dawnflower
- **Alignment Tendency:** Neutral Good
- **Domains:** Fire, Healing, Sun, Truth
- **Divine Font:** Heal
- **Divine Attribute:** Constitution or Wisdom
- **Favored Weapon:** Scimitar
- **Edicts:** Destroy the spawn of Rovagug, protect allies, provide aid to the sick and injured, seek and allow redemption
- **Anathema:** Create undead, fail to strike down evil, lie
- **Common Worshipers:** Healers, redeemers, farmers, people of the desert, the downtrodden
- **Holy Symbol:** Angelic ankh
- **Associated Nations:** Qadira, Osirion, Katapesh, Taldor (growing following)
- **Campaign Relevance:** Sun goddess of healing and redemption. Her church believes everyone deserves a chance at redemption — but those who refuse face the swift justice of her burning blade. Major divine healing source. Strong presence in desert nations with Keleshite influence.

---

### Shelyn — The Eternal Rose
- **Alignment Tendency:** Neutral Good
- **Domains:** Creation, Family, Passion, Protection
- **Divine Font:** Heal
- **Divine Attribute:** Constitution or Charisma
- **Favored Weapon:** Glaive
- **Edicts:** Be peaceful, choose and perfect an art, lead by example, see the beauty in all things
- **Anathema:** Destroy art or an object of beauty, repeat a design when creating art, refuse to googly help craft something meaningful
- **Common Worshipers:** Artists, poets, lovers, musicians, craftspeople
- **Holy Symbol:** Songbird with outstretched wings
- **Associated Nations:** Taldor (strong arts tradition), universal
- **Campaign Relevance:** Goddess of beauty, art, and love. Her brother Zon-Kuthon was corrupted — she carries his glaive as a reminder and hopes to redeem him. Non-violent faith, but her champions fight to protect beauty from destruction.

---

### Torag — The Forge Father
- **Alignment Tendency:** Lawful Good
- **Domains:** Creation, Earth, Family, Protection
- **Divine Font:** Heal
- **Divine Attribute:** Constitution or Wisdom
- **Favored Weapon:** Warhammer
- **Edicts:** Be honorable and forthright, keep your word, respect the forge, serve your people
- **Anathema:** Tell lies or cheat someone, intentionally create inferior works, show cowardice
- **Common Worshipers:** Dwarves, smiths, miners, engineers, defenders
- **Holy Symbol:** Iron hammer
- **Associated Nations:** Five Kings Mountains, dwarven communities everywhere
- **Campaign Relevance:** Primary dwarven deity. God of the forge, strategy, and protection. His church is deeply woven into dwarven society — every dwarven settlement has a Torag temple-forge. Non-dwarves worship him for craftsmanship and defensive warfare.

---

### Urgathoa — The Pallid Princess
- **Alignment Tendency:** Neutral Evil
- **Domains:** Indulgence, Magic, Might, Undeath
- **Divine Font:** Harm
- **Divine Attribute:** Constitution or Wisdom
- **Favored Weapon:** Scythe
- **Edicts:** Become combative when extravagance is threatened, extravagantly experience everything, pursue physical pleasures
- **Anathema:** Deny yourself, extinguish extravagance, be moderate in any way
- **Common Worshipers:** Undead, necromancers, gluttons, plague-cultists
- **Holy Symbol:** Skull-backed fly
- **Associated Nations:** Geb (undead-ruled nation), secret cults everywhere
- **Campaign Relevance:** First mortal to become undead — she escaped the Boneyard and brought undeath into the world. Patron of vampires, liches, and ghouls. Her cults spread disease and excess. Major villain patron. Diametrically opposed to Pharasma.

---

### Zon-Kuthon — The Midnight Lord
- **Alignment Tendency:** Lawful Evil
- **Domains:** Ambition, Darkness, Destruction, Pain
- **Divine Font:** Harm
- **Divine Attribute:** Constitution or Wisdom
- **Favored Weapon:** Spiked chain
- **Edicts:** Bring pain to the world, mutilate yourself and others, show devotion through suffering
- **Anathema:** Create crafts of beauty, provide comfort to the afflicted, allow someone to be healed of pain and suffering
- **Common Worshipers:** Masochists, sadists, torturers, shadow-aligned beings, Nidalese
- **Holy Symbol:** Skull with chains
- **Associated Nations:** Nidal (entire nation bound to him)
- **Campaign Relevance:** Shelyn's brother, corrupted by the dark spaces between the planes. His worship is mandatory in Nidal. Velstracs (pain-fiends) serve him. Campaigns involving Nidal, shadow magic, or pain themes will encounter his influence. Deeply personal evil — not conquest, but suffering.

---

### Gorum — Our Lord in Iron
- **Alignment Tendency:** Chaotic Neutral
- **Domains:** Confidence, Destruction, Might, Zeal
- **Divine Font:** Harm or Heal
- **Divine Attribute:** Strength or Constitution
- **Favored Weapon:** Greatsword
- **Edicts:** Attain victory in fair combat, push limits, train ceaselessly for battle
- **Anathema:** Kill prisoners or surrendering foes, prevent conflict through negotiation, win a battle through trickery
- **Common Worshipers:** Soldiers, mercenaries, barbarians, gladiators, warriors of all kinds
- **Holy Symbol:** Iron mountain with sword plunged in
- **Associated Nations:** Brevoy (Aldori swordsmanship), any martial culture
- **Campaign Relevance:** God of battle and strength. Not evil — he respects fair combat and honorable warriors. His churches are sparring halls. Clerics are battlefield chaplains. Popular among martial characters who want divine connection without moral strings.

---

### Rovagug — The Rough Beast
- **Alignment Tendency:** Chaotic Evil
- **Domains:** Air, Destruction, Earth, Zeal
- **Divine Font:** Harm
- **Divine Attribute:** Constitution or Strength
- **Favored Weapon:** Greataxe
- **Edicts:** Destroy all things, free Rovagug from his prison
- **Anathema:** Create something new, cooperate with anyone except to destroy
- **Common Worshipers:** Doomsday cultists, orcs (sometimes), gnolls (sometimes), the truly insane
- **Holy Symbol:** Open jaw with insects pouring out
- **Associated Nations:** None — universally reviled
- **Campaign Relevance:** God of destruction, imprisoned at the center of Golarion by every other deity working together. The Tarrasque and Spawn of Rovagug are his children. The only deity ALL other deities agree must stay imprisoned. Cults that worship Rovagug are universally hunted. The ultimate apocalyptic threat.

---

## Notable Dead/Changed Deities (Post-Godsrain)

### Aroden [DEAD — Died 4606 AR]
- **Was:** God of humanity, civilization, and prophecy
- **Died:** His death broke prophecy itself. The Worldwound opened, Cheliax fell to diabolism, and the Age of Lost Omens began.
- **Campaign Relevance:** His death is the defining event of the current age. Many plot hooks involve his legacy, missing artifacts, or the consequences of his fall. Iomedae inherited most of his following. Some cults believe he'll return.

### Arazni [CHANGED — Elevated during Godsrain]
- **Was:** Iomedae's predecessor as Aroden's herald, killed by the Whispering Tyrant, raised as a lich-queen of Geb
- **Now:** Freed from undeath during the Godsrain, elevated to true divinity. Goddess of the abused, the wronged, and survivors.
- **Domains:** Confidence, Protection, Freedom, Sorrow
- **Campaign Relevance:** A deity who was enslaved, killed, desecrated, and ultimately freed herself. Patron of abuse survivors and those reclaiming their autonomy.

---

## Tian Xia Deities (Major)

### Daikitsu — Lady of Foxes
- **Alignment Tendency:** Neutral
- **Domains:** Change, Creation, Family, Nature
- **Favored Weapon:** Flail
- **Key Aspects:** Kitsune patron, agriculture, craftsmanship, rice harvest
- **Campaign Relevance:** Major deity in Minkai and across Tian Xia. Her kitsune followers are shapeshifting foxfolk. Practical, nature-oriented worship focused on harvests and family.

### Fumeiyoshi — Lord of Envy
- **Alignment Tendency:** Neutral Evil
- **Domains:** Death, Destruction, Earth, Undeath
- **Favored Weapon:** Naginata
- **Key Aspects:** Envy, graves, undead, dishonor
- **Campaign Relevance:** Tian Xia's primary undead-patron deity. Envies his brother Tsukiyo. Fumeiyoshi cults raise undead and desecrate graves. Major villain patron in Tian Xia campaigns.

### General Susumu — The Black Daimyo
- **Alignment Tendency:** Lawful Evil
- **Domains:** Confidence, Destruction, Duty, Might
- **Favored Weapon:** Longbow
- **Key Aspects:** Dishonorable warfare, conquest, betrayal in battle
- **Campaign Relevance:** The dark mirror of honorable war. His followers believe victory justifies any means — poison, assassination, betrayal. Opposed by Shizuru's followers.

### Hei Feng — Duke of Thunder
- **Alignment Tendency:** Chaotic Neutral
- **Domains:** Air, Destruction, Indulgence, Water
- **Favored Weapon:** Jiu huan dao (nine-ring broadsword)
- **Key Aspects:** Sea storms, thunder, reckless courage, sailors
- **Campaign Relevance:** Popular among sailors and coastal peoples in Tian Xia. Bombastic and unpredictable. His storms can be blessings or disasters. Good patron for swashbuckler-types.

### Lady Nanbyo — The Widow of Suffering
- **Alignment Tendency:** Chaotic Evil
- **Domains:** Destruction, Fire, Earth, Water
- **Favored Weapon:** Warhammer
- **Key Aspects:** Natural disasters, earthquakes, tsunamis, plague
- **Campaign Relevance:** Feared across Tian Xia as the source of natural disasters. Not actively worshiped so much as desperately appeased. Her "temples" are disaster shrines where people pray to be spared.

### Nalinivati — The Serpent's Kiss
- **Alignment Tendency:** Neutral
- **Domains:** Charm, Magic, Nightmares, Secrecy
- **Favored Weapon:** Urumi (whip-sword)
- **Key Aspects:** Naga queen-goddess, sorcery, fertility, snakes
- **Campaign Relevance:** Primary deity of Nagajor. Naga worshipers and serpentfolk across Tian Xia venerate her. Her magic traditions are ancient and powerful.

### Qi Zhong — Master of Medicine
- **Alignment Tendency:** Neutral Good
- **Domains:** Healing, Knowledge, Magic, Protection
- **Favored Weapon:** Heavy mace
- **Key Aspects:** Medicine, healing, scholarly magic, alchemy
- **Campaign Relevance:** Tian Xia's primary healing deity. His temples are hospitals. Physician-priests combine divine magic with traditional medicine. Essential for any healer-focused character in Tian Xia.

### Shizuru — Empress of Heaven
- **Alignment Tendency:** Lawful Good
- **Domains:** Duty, Perfection, Sun, Vigil
- **Favored Weapon:** Katana
- **Key Aspects:** The sun, honor, swordplay, ancestors
- **Campaign Relevance:** Tian Xia's primary lawful good deity — equivalent to Iomedae's cultural role. Patron of samurai, honorable warriors, and those who serve with duty. Lover of Tsukiyo (the moon god). Central to Minkai and Shokuro culture.

### Sun Wukong — The Monkey King
- **Alignment Tendency:** Chaotic Neutral
- **Domains:** Indulgence, Might, Trickery, Freedom
- **Favored Weapon:** Bo staff
- **Key Aspects:** Trickery, rebellion, cleverness, freedom from authority
- **Campaign Relevance:** The beloved trickster-hero who defied heaven itself. Popular among rogues, monks, and anyone who loves a good prank. His worship is irreverent and joyful. Excellent patron for chaotic characters in Tian Xia.

### Tsukiyo — Prince of the Moon
- **Alignment Tendency:** Lawful Good
- **Domains:** Darkness, Moon, Soul, Vigil
- **Favored Weapon:** Longspear
- **Key Aspects:** The moon, jade, spirits, protection of the dead
- **Campaign Relevance:** Lover of Shizuru. Brother of Fumeiyoshi (who envies and hates him). Murdered and resurrected, which connects him to themes of death and renewal. Protects the spirits of the dead on their journey to the afterlife.

---

## Deity Quick Reference by Domain

### Healing & Protection
Pharasma, Sarenrae, Shelyn, Torag, Iomedae, Qi Zhong

### Magic & Knowledge
Nethys, Irori, Nalinivati, Qi Zhong

### Nature & Elements
Gozreh, Erastil, Desna, Hei Feng, Daikitsu

### War & Combat
Gorum, Iomedae, Shizuru, General Susumu

### Trickery & Shadow
Calistria, Norgorber, Sun Wukong

### Evil & Destruction
Lamashtu, Urgathoa, Zon-Kuthon, Rovagug, Fumeiyoshi, Lady Nanbyo

### Death & Undeath
Pharasma (anti-undead), Urgathoa (pro-undead), Fumeiyoshi (pro-undead)

### Freedom & Travel
Desna, Cayden Cailean, Sun Wukong

---

## Deity-Nation Associations (Quick Lookup)

| Nation | Primary Deity/Deities |
|--------|----------------------|
| Absalom | All (cosmopolitan) |
| Andoran | Cayden Cailean, Iomedae |
| Cheliax | Asmodeus (archdevil patron), Zon-Kuthon |
| Five Kings Mountains | Torag |
| Kyonin | Calistria, Desna |
| Mendev | Iomedae |
| Nidal | Zon-Kuthon (mandatory) |
| Nirmathas | Erastil |
| Osirion | Pharasma, Sarenrae, Nethys |
| Qadira | Sarenrae |
| Rahadoum | None (religion banned) |
| Ustalav | Pharasma, Desna |
| Varisia | Desna (Varisians) |
| Minkai | Shizuru, Tsukiyo, Daikitsu |
| Hongal | Desna, Gorum |
| Hwanggot | Shizuru, Irori, ancestor worship |
| Nagajor | Nalinivati |
| Xa Hoi | Sovereign dragons (minimal deity worship) |
